name: Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra
name_original: Pico Urbión – trail running circular desde la Laguna Negra (Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión)
region: Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbion
description: This difficult 8.3-mile jogging loop from Laguna Negra offers stunning views and significant elevation gain to Pico Urbión.
region_intro: Jogging in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbión offers diverse terrain shaped by ancient glacial activity. The region features impressive glacial cirques, several lagoons including the prominent Laguna Negra, and the high peak of Pico de Urbión. Runners can experience varied landscapes, from extensive Scots pine forests in the valleys to high mountain pastures at higher altitudes, providing a range of surfaces for running. Best jogging routes in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbión The most popular jogging route is Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete, offering challenging climbs towards the peak and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas – trail running circular por el Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión, a difficult 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path that provides a circular experience through the natural park's varied terrain. Local runners also love the Laguna Helada – Laguna Negra de Urbión loop from Refugio de El Becedo, a 12.1 miles (19.5 km) trail leading through multiple glacial lagoons, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Jogging in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbión is defined by glacial cirques, serene lagoons, and dense pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from paths around the lagoons to challenging ascents. The routes in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión, offering a wide range of experiences. These trails are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review, and more than 200 runners have explored the varied terrain using komoot.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in this region?
A: The region's unique glacial origins mean you'll encounter diverse terrain. Expect everything from shaded paths through extensive Scots pine forests in the valleys to open, elevated trails across high mountain pastures. The routes often feature impressive glacial cirques and serene lagoons, providing varied surfaces for your run.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: While many routes in the area are rated moderate to difficult, the landscape offers options for various fitness levels. For instance, paths around the lagoons provide a less strenuous experience. The region's trail network allows you to choose routes that match your desired intensity, from gentle slopes to more challenging ascents.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see while running?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter stunning glacial cirques and several enchanting lagoons, including the famous Laguna Negra. Highlights include the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;, and other beautiful lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt;. The landscape also features dense pine forests and high mountain pastures.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: Yes, the area offers several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064170909/sierra-al-pico-urbion-picacho-del-camperon-y-penas-blancas-trail-running-circular-por-el-parque-natural-laguna-negra-y-circos-glaciares-de-urbion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas – trail running circular por el Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 5.1-mile (8.2 km) path. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Helada – Laguna Negra de Urbión loop from Refugio de El Becedo&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.1-mile (19.5 km) trail leading through multiple glacial lagoons.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: Many trails in natural parks are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Please check local regulations or signage at trailheads for specific rules regarding pets in certain areas.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: The best season for jogging typically depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, with vibrant flora in spring and stunning colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter brings snow and ice, making some higher-altitude trails challenging and requiring specialized gear.

Q: Are there any routes that offer challenging climbs and panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trail runners seeking an intense workout and breathtaking vistas, the ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; offers challenging terrain and panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064155512/pico-urbion-loop-from-laguna-negra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.3-mile (13.4 km) trail that provides challenging climbs towards the peak.

Q: Is there parking available for accessing the running routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at designated spots, such as Paso de la Serrá. For some areas, like the final stretch to Laguna Negra, you might need to park and then walk or take a bus, especially during peak seasons. It's advisable to check specific access points for parking information before your visit.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls or rivers?
A: Yes, the landscape is crisscrossed by numerous streams and the nascent Duero River, adding to the scenic beauty. The Arroyo de La Laguna, with its beautiful waterfall, is a notable attraction, especially on the way to Pico de Urbión, providing picturesque spots along your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the stunning natural beauty, from the dark, mysterious waters of Laguna Negra to the expansive views from Pico de Urbión. The diverse trail network and varied terrain, offering options for all abilities, are also frequently highlighted as inspiring and rewarding.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes are challenging, the park offers paths suitable for various levels. Relatively flat paths around the lagoons, such as those skirting Laguna Negra, can be enjoyable for families. These provide inspiring scenery without overly demanding terrain, allowing for a pleasant shared experience.
tips: The black lagoon is located north of Soria, in the well-known region of Pinares. Discover this spectacular lagoon of glacial origin located at almost 2000 meters of altitude in the Sierra de Urbión. There are several legends about the black lagoon since it has no bottom, that it communicates with the sea through a series of caves and currents or that a being lives in its dark waters that devours everyone who falls into it. The most widespread legend is the one written by Antonio Machado in 1912 in the Land of Alvar Gonzalez, alluding to the absence of a background. The reality is that the black lagoon of Urbión is between 8 and 10 meters deep.

Laguna Larga is one of the glacial lagoons found in the Urbión Mountains, very close to the famous Laguna Negra, in the province of Soria. It's less well-known but equally impressive, and is part of a natural environment of great beauty and ecological value.

Pico de Urbión is a mountain located in the Sierra de Urbión, on the border between the provinces of Soria and La Rioja. It is a popular destination for nature lovers and hikers.
The natural beauty of the Sierra de Urbión offers excellent opportunities for photography.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Pico Urbión Loop for jogging?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a challenging trail run, requiring very good fitness. It involves significant elevation gain (over 600 meters) and sections that are steep, rocky, and technical. Sure-footedness and sturdy trail running shoes are essential.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and demanding. You'll encounter sections with loose stones, roots, and potentially muddy areas, especially in spring. As you ascend towards Pico Urbión, the path becomes rugged and steep, transitioning to an alpine plateau and rocky landscapes. It includes both mountain hiking paths and technical alpine sections.

faqs.question: What natural landmarks or viewpoints will I see on this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts at the iconic Laguna Negra de Urbión, a glacial lake steeped in legend. As you ascend, you'll pass other glacial lakes like Laguna Larga and Laguna Helada. The summit of Pico de Urbión offers unparalleled panoramic views of the Sierra de la Demanda, the Iberian System, and the Duero plains. You'll also be near the source of the Duero River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this loop is typically from late spring to early autumn (June to October) when snow has melted and weather conditions are more stable. Winter brings significant challenges due to snow and ice, making it suitable only for experienced individuals with appropriate gear.

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trailhead for the Pico Urbión Loop starts at Laguna Negra de Urbión. There is parking available near Laguna Negra, but it can fill up quickly, especially during peak season. Arriving early is highly recommended to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Urbión Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Natural Park of Laguna Negra and Glacial Cirques of Urbión, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on the challenging, rocky terrain. Be mindful of their endurance on such a demanding route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There is typically no specific permit required for hiking or jogging this route within the Natural Park of Laguna Negra and Glacial Cirques of Urbión. However, there might be a small fee for vehicle access to the parking area near Laguna Negra during peak times. Always check local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging loop?
faqs.answer: The tour data suggests an average duration of around 2 hours and 37 minutes (9428 seconds) for jogging. However, given the difficult terrain and significant elevation, this can vary greatly depending on your fitness level, pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the views. Allow ample time, especially if you're not accustomed to high-altitude trail running.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this trail run?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging terrain and high-mountain environment, you should bring sturdy trail running shoes with good grip, layers of clothing suitable for changing weather, a windproof/waterproof jacket, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot), and sun protection. Trekking poles can also be beneficial on steep sections.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or children?
faqs.answer: The full Pico Urbión Loop is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginners due to its significant difficulty, steep ascents, and technical terrain. However, the initial paths around Laguna Negra de Urbión are much easier and can be enjoyed by families looking for a shorter, less strenuous walk.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that are part of the "Covaleda-Pico urbión" trail, and smaller portions of the "[CIMA SO04] Laguna Negra de Urbión" and "Etapa 01: Fuentes del Duero – Vinuesa" trails.
